questions.question: Prove that ¬(p ∧ q) ≡ ¬p ∨ ¬q using the laws of logic. What logical operation do you apply first?
Which law of logic states that (p → q) ∧ (q → p) ≡ p ∧ q?
Show that (p ∨ q) ∧ ¬p ≡ ψ for any proposition ψ, using the laws of logic. What does this equivalence say about the statement p ∨ q?
questions.hint: Apply the law of excluded middle and distributive law
